title: Shri Kedarnath Temple, Uttarakhand
intro: The Shri Kedarnath Temple, situated in the Rudraprayag district of Uttarakhand, is one of the most sacred temples in India dedicated to Lord Shiva.
description:
Shri Kedarnath Temple – Uttarakhand
Introduction
The Shri Kedarnath Temple, situated in the Rudraprayag district of Uttarakhand, is one of the most sacred temples in India dedicated to Lord Shiva. It is one of the 12 Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva and holds a prominent place in the famous Char Dham Yatra and the Panch Kedar.
Nestled amidst the high mountain ranges of the Himalayas, Kedarnath Dham is renowned for its spiritual significance, natural beauty, and arduous pilgrimage. Every year, lakhs of devotees arrive here to seek the blessings of Lord Kedarnath.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Kedarnath Temple
 Other Names: Kedarnath Dham, Kedareshwar Temple
 Location: Kedarnath, Rudraprayag District, Uttarakhand 246445, India
 Presiding Deity: Lord Shiva (Jyotirlinga form)
 Significance: One of the 12 major Jyotirlingas
 Nearest Major City: Dehradun (approx. 250 km from Gaurikund)
History and Significance
The Kedarnath Temple is considered one of the most ancient and sacred Shiva shrines. According to religious beliefs, the Pandavas worshipped Lord Shiva here, and many mythological legends are associated with this region.
The temple is situated at an altitude of approximately 3,583 meters above sea level and is surrounded by snow-capped Himalayan mountains. Devotees consider the pilgrimage to Kedarnath Dham a symbol of their devotion and dedication to Lord Shiva.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air
 Jolly Grant Airport, Dehradun: Distance from Gaurikund is approx. 238 km. Taxis and buses are available from the airport to reach Gaurikund.
🚆 By Train
 Rishikesh Railway Station: Distance from Gaurikund is approx. 210 km. Gaurikund can be reached from Rishikesh by road.
🛣️ By Road
Travelers reach Gaurikund by road. From there, a trek of approximately 16 kilometers is required to reach the Kedarnath Temple.
Facilities available during the journey:
 Trekking path
 Horse/Mule service
 Palki (Palanquin) service
 Helicopter service (subject to availability during the season)
Best Time to Visit
The Kedarnath Temple remains closed in winter due to heavy snowfall.
Best time for the Yatra:
 May to June
 September to October
The weather is relatively favorable during these months.
Main Festivals
 Kapat Opening Ceremony: The temple doors open on the auspicious occasion of Akshaya Tritiya.
 Badri-Kedar Festival: A festival associated with the religious traditions of Badrinath and Kedarnath Dham.
 Kapat Closing Ceremony: The temple doors are closed for the winter season around the festival of Bhai Dooj.
Temple Timings
🕒 4:00 AM to 9:00 PM
(General timings during the pilgrimage season. Timings may change according to special occasions and weather conditions.)
Nearby Attractions
 Bhairavnath Temple: An important religious site of Kedarnath Dham. It is believed that Lord Bhairavnath protects this region during winter.
 Vasuki Tal: A beautiful natural lake situated at a high altitude.
 Gandhi Sarovar: A place famous for its natural beauty and peaceful environment.
 Gaurikund: The main starting point of the Kedarnath Yatra.
Dress Code
Given the cold and high altitude in Kedarnath:
 Wear warm clothes.
 Carry jackets, woolens, and rain protection gear.
 Wear comfortable trekking shoes.
Facilities for Devotees
 Yatra registration facility
 Accommodation arrangements
 Food and water facilities
 Medical assistance centers
 Helicopter service
 Security arrangements
Conclusion
The Shri Kedarnath Temple is one of the holiest abodes of Lord Shiva. Nestled in the lap of the Himalayas, this temple is a wonderful center of faith, penance, and spiritual experience. The Kedarnath Yatra is not just a pilgrimage, but a spiritual journey of faith, patience, and dedication towards Lord Shiva for the devotees.

faq.q1: To which deity is the Kedarnath Temple dedicated?
faq.a1: The Kedarnath Temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and is one of the 12 Jyotirlingas.
faq.q2: Where is the Kedarnath Temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in the Rudraprayag district of Uttarakhand.
faq.q3: What is the nearest railway station to reach Kedarnath?
faq.a3: Rishikesh Railway Station is the nearest major railway station.
faq.q4: How much trekking is required to reach the Kedarnath Temple?
faq.a4: One has to trek approximately 16 kilometers from Gaurikund.
faq.q5: When does the Kedarnath Temple open?
faq.a5: The temple typically opens around Akshaya Tritiya and closes around Bhai Dooj.
